Description: Topic taxonomy and context type registry for the EIP pipeline. Version 3.1 adds Decision, Action, Commitment, and Escalation context types, topic group metadata, and short descriptions for all topics.

changes: ContextTypes: added Decision, Action, Commitment, Escalation
ContextTypeDescriptions added for all 11 context types
TopicGroups added: Product, Delivery, Financial, Operational, Strategic
Topic descriptions added for all 18 topics
Initial taxonomy with 18 topics and 7 context types

T01.Name: Product Performance
T01.Description: Functional performance, UX quality, scan accuracy, speed, battery, thermal behaviour

T02.Name: Product Quality & Compliance
T02.Description: Regulatory compliance, certification, defects, quality standards, safety

T03.Name: Product Value & Perception
T03.Description: Customer feedback, usability, value perception, market appeal

T04.Name: Product Scope & Prioritisation
T04.Description: Feature scope, backlog, MVP definition, requirements, prioritisation decisions

T05.Name: Delivery Progress & Readiness
T05.Description: Launch readiness, milestones, deployment, rollout scheduling and timing

T06.Name: Delivery Risk & Constraints
T06.Description: Blockers, delays, constraints, stalled progress, bottlenecks

T07.Name: Development Execution
T07.Description: Engineering execution, sprint progress, build, code, development activity

T08.Name: Cash Flow & Liquidity
T08.Description: Cash position, liquidity, funding runway, capital requirements

T09.Name: Cost Structure & Margins
T09.Description: Cost base, margins, cost overruns, unit economics, BOM cost

T10.Name: Revenue & Commercial Performance
T10.Description: Revenue performance, sales results, commercial outcomes, pricing

T11.Name: Financial Risk & Exposure
T11.Description: Financial exposure, foreign exchange, credit risk, financial contingency

T12.Name: Organisation & Capability
T12.Description: Org structure, headcount, team capability, hiring, skills gaps

T13.Name: Resource Allocation
T13.Description: Resource assignment, capacity, bandwidth, competing priorities

T14.Name: Operational Effectiveness
T14.Description: Process efficiency, operational performance, supply chain, logistics

T15.Name: Strategic Direction & Alignment
T15.Description: Strategic priorities, vision, direction-setting, alignment across leadership

T16.Name: Product-Market Fit
T16.Description: Market positioning, competitive landscape, TAM, segments, product-market alignment

T17.Name: Growth & Opportunities
T17.Description: Growth initiatives, new markets, partnerships, commercial opportunities

T18.Name: Delivery Confidence
T18.Description: Overall confidence in delivery, cross-topic risk sentiment, leadership posture

Product.Description: Topics concerning product quality, scope, performance and customer perception

Delivery.Description: Topics concerning engineering execution, delivery risk and launch readiness

Financial.Description: Topics concerning cash, cost, revenue and financial risk

Operational.Description: Topics concerning organisation, resources, capability and operational effectiveness

Strategic.Description: Topics concerning direction, market fit, growth and cross-portfolio confidence

ContextTypeDescriptions.Strategy: High-level direction, priorities, goals and alignment discussions
ContextTypeDescriptions.Execution: Progress updates, delivery activity, sprint work and implementation
ContextTypeDescriptions.Problem: Issues, defects, failures, and things that are not working as expected
ContextTypeDescriptions.Risk: Potential future problems, uncertainty, exposure and mitigation
ContextTypeDescriptions.Opportunity: Growth, improvement or commercial possibilities identified in discussion
ContextTypeDescriptions.Governance: Approvals, compliance, oversight, policy and process decisions
ContextTypeDescriptions.Learning: Retrospective insights, experiments, lessons learned and knowledge sharing
ContextTypeDescriptions.Decision: A clear decision reached or ratified in the meeting — with owner and rationale
ContextTypeDescriptions.Action: A specific task assigned to a named owner with expected completion
ContextTypeDescriptions.Commitment: A formal commitment made by an individual or team to a deliverable or outcome
ContextTypeDescriptions.Escalation: An issue, risk or decision explicitly escalated to a higher authority for resolution
